methods.description: This method examines the ability of bumblebees to gather pollen from two different plant species with distinct flower angles. The experiment involves observing bee behavior and collecting pollen samples under controlled conditions.

steps.description: Two captive colonies of naïve bees were obtained from Koppert© (Nové Zámky, Slovakia).
steps.searchable_summary: obtain captive colonies of naïve bumblebees from supplier

steps.description: Bees were kept at 22-24°C in a room lit by natural light and neon light (370 lx).
steps.searchable_summary: maintain bees in controlled temperature and lighting conditions

steps.description: Bees were connected to an insectarium by a plastic mesh tube and daily fed exclusively with honey solution (water 60% and honey 40%).
steps.searchable_summary: connect bees to insectarium and feed with honey solution

steps.description: Avoided pollen feeding to prevent bee contamination with pollen grains.
steps.searchable_summary: prevent pollen feeding to avoid contamination

steps.description: Individually tested bees in the insectarium by placing one freshly collected snowdrop/lesser celandine in flower glass test tubes.
steps.searchable_summary: test bees with fresh flowers in insectarium

steps.description: When the bee was feeding inside the flower for 5 seconds, it was quickly removed and fixed in 70% ethanol for further examination of pollen loads.
steps.searchable_summary: remove and fix bees in ethanol after feeding for pollen examination

steps.description: Removed bees that flew away from the flower earlier from further experiments.
steps.searchable_summary: exclude bees that did not feed properly from experiments

steps.description: Each flower was used only once.
steps.searchable_summary: ensure each flower is used once in experiments

steps.description: If the bee did not feed on the flower after 5 minutes, the trial was terminated, and the bee was tested again on one of the following days.
steps.searchable_summary: terminate trial if bee does not feed and retest later
